QF.id: I Clearing Settlement and Risk Management_61

question.text: What is the minimum Liquid Net Worth that every clearing member in the Currency Derivatives segment is required to maintain?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The text states: "Currently every clearing member in the Currency Derivatives segment is required to maintain a minimum liquid networth of Rs.50 lakhs." **The Trap:** Rs. 10 lakhs is the BMC for proprietary-only (no algo) trading members. Rs. 1 crore is the Committee-recommended net worth for trading members. Rs. 50 lakhs is specifically the minimum liquid net worth for CMs. **Cross-Exam Context:** Liquid Net Worth = Liquid Assets minus Initial Margin minus Extreme Loss Margin at any point in time. The CMs must maintain at least Rs. 50 lakhs of this at all times. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clearing Member minimum liquid net worth = Rs. 50 lakhs. This is distinct from the BMC (which has no exposure granted against it) and net worth requirements.
